zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Darrel於大約1405年出生在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾金頓時，她的父親Paul Dayrell27歲，母親Margaret Priscot22歲。她於1447年之前與Sir Richard Pigot在英國巴金哈姆爾蕾托 霍武德結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1510年在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾蕾托 霍武德享年106歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Darrel于大约1405年出生在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾金頓时，她的父亲Paul Dayrell27岁，母亲Margaret Priscot22岁。她于1447年之前与Sir Richard Pigot在英国巴金哈姆爾蕾托 霍武德结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1510年在英国英格兰巴金哈姆爾蕾托 霍武德享年106岁。

nameMeanings.name: Darrel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish and English: variant of   Carrell  .

French: from Old French quar(r)el, carrel ‘bolt (for a crossbow)’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a maker of crossbow bolts or figuratively a nickname for a short, stout man (compare English Carrell ). The word also meant ‘paving slab’, and so it could also have been a metonymic occupational name for a street layer.

variants.name: Carrell
meanings: English: variant of Quarrell, either a topographic or occupational name for someone who lived by or worked at a quarry (Middle English quarrel), synonymous with Quarry , or a nickname from Middle English, Old French quarel, quarrel, carrel, denoting a short, heavy, square-headed bolt for a cross-bow, arbalest, or siege engine; this use might have had an occupational connotation, for someone who made or used quarrels, or it may have been used figuratively for a thickset or stout person. In Devon and Hampshire the name Quarrell survived as Carol and Car(r)oll. In Yorkshire and Durham from the late 18th century onward the name became indistinguishable from Carroll and its variants, borne by Irish migrants.

English (of Norman origin): perhaps also a Norman French habitational name from Carelles in Mayenne (France), early recorded as Quarrel. Compare Carrel . No medieval bearers have been found in England to confirm this origin.

variants.name: Carreau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Donat, Marcelle, Pierre, Yves.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Darrel was born about 1405, in Kington, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Paul Dayrell, was 27 and her mother, Margaret Priscot, was 22. She married Sir Richard Pigot before 1447, in Little Horwood, Buckinghamshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died in 1510, in Little Horwood, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 106.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Darrel est née environ 1405, à Kington, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Paul Dayrell, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Margaret Priscot, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Richard Pigot avant 1447, à Little Horwood, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1510, à Little Horwood, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 106 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Darrel(이)가 대략 1405년 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 킨그턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Paul Dayrell(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Priscot(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1447년 이전 영국 벅잉함실르 리틀 호우드에서 Sir Richard Pigot(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1510년 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르 리틀 호우드에서 106세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Darrel родилась около 1405 г. в местности кингтон, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Paul Dayrell было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Priscot, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Richard Pigot перед 1447 г. в местности литл хаурвуд, буккингхамшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1510 г. в местности литл хаурвуд, буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 106 лет.
